name: Copy Then Zero Rows and Columns
summary: Snapshot the matrix, then for every zero in the snapshot blank its whole row and column in place.
visualizationIntent: init: the matrix is shown and a snapshot grid original pops in beside it; loop(scan): the scan cell (row,col) is current; the snapshot marks its zeros special; check: when original[row][col] is 0 we act; update(zero): the whole row then the whole column in matrix are blanked, cells flipping to result (0); return: matrix holds every affected row and column zeroed.

lineExplanations.5: Prepare an empty snapshot of the matrix.
lineExplanations.6: Walk each row of the original matrix.
lineExplanations.7: Store a copy of that row so later edits don't corrupt our reads.
lineExplanations.10: If the snapshot cell here was a zero, act on it.
lineExplanations.12: Blank the cell in this row.
lineExplanations.14: Blank the cell in this column.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class the platform runs.
syntaxExplanations.2: def setZeroes(self, matrix): the method receiving the matrix.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(matrix) is the number of rows.
syntaxExplanations.4: len(matrix[0]) is the number of columns.
syntaxExplanations.5: [] is an empty list for the snapshot.
syntaxExplanations.6: for source_row in matrix iterates the actual row lists.
syntaxExplanations.7: source_row[:] makes a shallow copy of the row; .append stores it.
syntaxExplanations.8: for row in range(rows) iterates row indices.
syntaxExplanations.9: for col in range(cols) iterates column indices.
syntaxExplanations.11: for current_col in range(cols) walks the whole row.
syntaxExplanations.13: for current_row in range(rows) walks the whole column.
syntaxExplanations.15: return matrix hands back the modified grid.
